reviews.review_title: Great boxing training, with a caveat
reviews.review_description:
I'm an avid rhythm gamer, and a fan of both Beat Saber and of the Les Mills Bodycombat app for fun and for workouts, so I know what I'm talking about when I recommend Beat the Beats. The game looks great, the moves are intuitive and detect well, and it's nice to see your power score for each punch live as you throw em!

My one sticking point is that, unlike the Les Mills app, this game has you in a left or right combat stance the whole time, never having you switch sides, which can lead to overtraining. I would also love to see more squat moves incorporated, because as it stands right now, this is primarily a workout for your arms, back, and core, not your full body as beat saber and the Les Mills app can be. For now, if you're using this as a part of your workout program, I suggest playing in front stance until a change is made so that each song works each side of your body equally.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the kind words and for the detailed feedback. We really appreciate it!

You’re right about the stance and workout balance. In our design, we prioritized fun and gameplay over a structured workout, so we focused on playful mechanics like blocks and dodges rather than specific exercise-driven movements.

That said, there’s no penalty for stance, so switching sides during a song -as you suggest- is a good way to balance the workout. You can also invert levels using the left-handed option in the settings.

About lower-body moves, sounds interesting. If something fun comes up that fits the gameplay, we might explore it in the future.

Thanks again for the support and for recommending the game!

reviews.review_description:
I think this game is the boxing game to co-exist alongside beatsaber 's... Sword slashing? A staple for every quest library. I've been waiting for it since I first played the demo on repeat many times. Buy this game asap (Or try the demo if you don't believe me). I'd love to be able to punch without the score popping up (weird that there's currently no way to turn this off but in the video it's not there) and different difficulties for each song would add a lot to the replayability. In its current state there's still a ton to do and devs seem active.

EDIT: Every album has a b-side which is just the same songs on a harder difficulty so there are actually different difficulties! 🥊

reviews.review_title: The Gold Standard of Fitness Rhythm Game
reviews.review_description:
I'm so thrilled to see a game finally launch that perfectly blends a sense of rhythm with fitness elements!
If you're curious about the actual gameplay, check it out here: https://youtube.com/shorts/378AMBYqQZQ?feature=share
With 30 base tracks included, the volume of content is quite substantial.
Usually, focusing on the workout means sacrificing the rhythm, and focusing on the rhythm reduces the exercise intensity, but this game has struck that balance beautifully.
The sophisticated UI and simple yet flashy visual effects are incredibly impressive!
It also supports various languages for user convenience, and the "impact feel" is excellent.
Since it’s still an early build, there are some minor bugs and it lacks features like a Custom Mode or MR Mode, but I really hope to see those added in future updates.

reviews.review_title: The game is good, but needs to be ironed
reviews.review_description:
￼ I would 100% recommend this title however don’t expect it to be flawless like Les Mills body combat

￼ It’s not uncommon for me to glitch through at least one object per match  max 2 per match 

￼ the dodging mechanic is pretty good, but there are ways to make it more fun. I would say.￼

￼also I have to slow my arm down because it’s glitching through objects 

But the game has excellent potential and I will keep playing

 I’m looking forward to improve improvements 



￼ update for comment below, all my detection issues happened with a left jab, I’m not getting this issue in any other game like haymaker or Les Mill so I don’t think it’s my controller, and I literally see my hand going through the box 
￼
￼

developer_response.body:
Hi Atlamus,

Sorry to hear you’re having issues with hit detection, that’s really frustrating. We spent a lot of time polishing this area, so your feedback is super valuable, especially since it’s the first report we’ve had since launch.

We’re committed to fixing it and keeping the quality as high as possible. Could you help us narrow it down?
 • Does it happen with all elements, or only with punches or blocks?
 • If it’s punches, does it affect a specific movement?
 • Is it possible that the element was hit too late?


If you have any additional feedback, feel free to reach out via email or join us on Discord. We really want to make the game a great experience.

Best,
Lucas

reviews.review_description: A very engaging VR rhythm boxing experience with smooth gameplay and great music. Highly accessible with customizable options, yet challenging enough to keep you coming back. A perfect mix of fitness and fun!

Love the inclusion of Meta Quest Achievements as I've been looking forward to some way to stay motivated with a rhythm game after completing Audica and Beat Saber. The hits feel solid and on beat, and I can't wait to hop back in! Will be buying all DLC for this one!
I was playing for only 5 mins and i was bored good game tho

I look forward to upgrading this rating to a four or five in the future. I decided to take a chance, given the reasonable price. It is possible that the less than great experience I had was related to being tall, or possibly to being physically limited in the ability to duck during the game. The controllers had to be held in a very specific rotation, kind of weird. The elements to be punch come weirdly close. I feel like I'm poking rather than punching. And I did try adjusting the height setting. The intimidating shapes that come at you to be ducked under require me to duck down at a level that is physically impossible me. So I never even got past the first level. Can't play the game if I can't duck under these weird shapes I guess. I'll try again in a month or two and see if it's improved.

Hello, developers.
I've been really looking forward to this game's release. I really wanted hand tracking in this game.
Is it really impossible to implement?
I'm so upset
